title: Deep contextualized word representations
authors: Matthew E. Peters, Mark Neumann, Mohit Iyyer, Matt Gardner et al.
hook: ELMo lets computers sense that bank means money in one sentence and river in another.
promise: You will understand how ELMo gives words context-sensitive meaning using deep language models.
prerequisite_note: It helps to know that words can have different meanings depending on the sentence.

glossary.plain: A way to represent words so their meaning changes with surrounding words.
A neural network that reads sentences both forwards and backwards to understand context.
When a single word has multiple meanings, like 'bat' as an animal or a baseball tool.
A model first trained on lots of text before being used for a specific task.
Hidden layers inside a neural network that capture complex features about the data.
A language problem like question answering that uses learned word representations.

cards.title: Words That Change With Context
body: Discover how ELMo lets computers read words as flexibly as humans do.

cards.title: Why bank means money or river
body: Imagine reading the word bank in two sentences: 'She deposited cash at the bank' and 'He sat by the river bank.'
Humans easily sense which bank is meant, but computers once struggled. ELMo changed that by making word meaning depend on context.

cards.title: How ELMo brings words to life
body: First, ELMo reads huge amounts of text and learns to predict words in both directions.
Then, it captures the inner workings of its neural network to create word representations that shift with context.
cards.caption: ELMo's process lets word meaning adapt to each sentence's context.

cards.title: See how context changes meaning
body: Compare how older models and ELMo handle the word 'plant' in two different sentences.
cards.offNote: Always gives 'plant' the same vector, missing factory versus flowerpot.
cards.onNote: Gives 'plant' a different vector for 'factory' and 'flower' sentences.

cards.title: How ELMo learns context
body: ELMo uses a deep bi-directional language model that reads each sentence both forwards and backwards.
It then combines signals from different layers of this network, so each word's representation changes based on the words around it.

cards.title: How much context do you want?
body: Slide to see how word meaning changes as you give ELMo more or less context.
cards.low: Words get a single, fixed meaning, regardless of surroundings.
cards.mid: Meaning shifts a bit, but not enough for tricky words.
cards.high: Meaning fully adapts to each sentence, capturing subtle differences.

cards.title: Choose what makes ELMo work
body: Select the six key ingredients that let ELMo create context-aware word representations.

choices.label: Bidirectional language model
choices.note: Reads text both forward and backward.

choices.label: Pre-training on large corpus
choices.note: Learns general language patterns before fine-tuning.

choices.label: Layer mixing
choices.note: Combines information from several neural network layers.

choices.label: Context-dependent embeddings
choices.note: Gives each word a meaning that adapts to its sentence.

choices.label: Character-level inputs
choices.note: Handles new or rare words by looking at characters.

choices.label: Plug-and-play for tasks
choices.note: Easily adds to existing NLP models.

cards.title: ELMo's leap in NLP benchmarks
body: ELMo quickly pushed benchmark-leading results across several language tasks, showing huge improvements over older word embeddings.
It proved that deep context helps models understand language more like humans do.
cards.caption: ELMo set new records on multiple NLP tasks by using deep context.

cards.title: Why ELMo is a milestone
body: ELMo marked the first time machines could sense word meaning in context, not just in isolation.
This opened the door for even more powerful models like BERT and GPT that now dominate language AI.

recap.label: Read
recap.note: Scans text both forwards and backwards.

recap.label: Capture
recap.note: Takes deep signals from multiple neural layers.

recap.label: Adapt
recap.note: Shifts each word's meaning based on its sentence.

recap.label: Boost
recap.note: Improves performance on many NLP tasks.

cards.title: Deep contextualized word representations
body: Explore the paper to see how ELMo shaped the future of language models.
